title: Blackline 24"x36" Twisted-Loop Car Drying Towel, 1300GSM, Streak Free, Lint Free, Reusable - Car Accessories, Microfiber Cleaning Cloth for Car, Truck, SUV - One Pass is All You Need
bullets: NO WATER SPOTS OR STREAKS - Only one pass and your car will be 100% dry, streak-free and will have zero water spots. Our Twisted Loop Technology allows the towel to glide across the surface of your car and pick up every single drop of water. It can absorb up to an entire gallon!

SIZE MATTERS - The Blackline Drying Towel is 2ft x 3ft allowing it to quickly dry the surface area of your vehicle. Not only is it huge, but it glides across your vehicle without scratching the paint at all. Size does matter when it comes to car drying towels.

ULTRA-ABSORBENT & EFFECTIVE - We designed the Blackline Drying Towel to be built for speed and performance. This towel strives to be the most absorbent, quick-drying towel on the car detailing market. It absorbs more than 6x its weight in water and can dry your entire car in one go!

ONE TOWEL IS ALL YOU NEED - Say goodbye to using towel after towel. It might be crazy to think about, but one pass is all you need with the Blackline Drying Towel. Your car will be completely dry and all you need is a single towel.

APPROVED BY PRO DETAILERS - Used and loved by professional car detailers and weekend warriors alike. It was specifically designed to be easy to use and to guarantee an effective drying experience every single time.

specs.label: Other Special Features of the Product

items.title: Streak Free Shine
items.body: This is the best drying towel I have used yet. Makes light work of drying a car in no time. Cleans large panels quickly and streak free. Definitely recommend. Read more Read less
items.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2026

items.title: Perfect Drying Towel – Worth Every Penny
items.body: I’ve used a lot of microfiber drying towels over the years, but the Blackline Car Drying Towel is easily the best I’ve come across. The size (2ft x 3ft) is perfect — large enough to cover a big section of the car in one pass, but still easy to handle without dragging on the ground. The 1300 GSM thickness is no joke; it feels plush and premium right out of the package. What impressed me most is just how much water this thing absorbs. After a full wash on my sedan, it had no problem drying the entire car without needing to be wrung out once. Even on glass and mirrors, it leaves no streaks, no spots, and definitely no scratches. I used to spend extra time chasing water drips around the trim, but this towel pulls everything up cleanly the first time. The edges are soft and reinforced, so I’m confident it won’t fray or scratch over time. I also appreciate that it washes well — no lint, no loss of absorbency after a few uses. It feels like a professional-grade towel but at a fair price, which explains why so many detailers recommend it. If you care about your car’s finish, this is the towel to get. It’s one of those detailing tools that instantly makes the process easier and more enjoyable. I won’t go back to cheap, thin towels after using this. Highly recommend! Read more Read less
items.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on September 8, 2025

items.title: Good towel. Not perfect. If you expect a single pass spotless dry, this misses a bit.
items.body: I bought the Blackline drying towel. For my first pull on my hood after a wash. It works. I dried the entire car with one towel. Absorption is strong and it feels soft on the paint. The performance falls short of the hype. It leaves light streaks in certain areas. You can see it in my video. I had to make a second pass on panels to get a clean finish. What I noticed: • Great water pickup on the first pass • Safe on paint with no scratching • Large size makes drying fast • Streaking on darker panels • Requires a quick follow up wipe in spots Good towel. Not perfect. If you expect a single pass spotless dry, this misses a bit. If you are fine doing a quick second wipe, it does the job well. Read more Read less
items.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on February 15, 2026

items.title: Best drying towel ever!!
items.body: 10/10 product. Definitely is good as advertised. Love drying my car with it, holds a lot of water, and is very big! It takes me a lot less time to dry my car after a long time of washing. Also the towel is extremely soft on my cars paint and doesn’t leave any micro scratches. Read more Read less
items.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on March 10, 2026

items.title: Only towel I’ll use on my cars!
items.body: Overall: The Blackline Drying Towel is an excellent car drying tool. It's highly absorbent, incredibly soft, and delivers a streak-free finish. While it's a bit pricier than some alternatives, the superior performance and quality make it a worthwhile investment for anyone serious about car detailing. If you're looking for a top-tier drying towel, I highly recommend the Blackline. It has definitely earned a place in my car care arsenal. Pros: Absorbency: This towel is seriously thirsty! The 1300 GSM (grams per square meter) rating definitely lives up to the hype. It soaks up a ton of water, making quick work of drying even large vehicles. I found I needed to wring it out far less frequently than with other microfiber towels I've used. Size: The XL size is fantastic. It's big enough to cover a large area quickly, reducing the number of passes needed and making the drying process much more efficient. It also makes it easier to handle and fold. Softness: The microfiber is incredibly soft and plush. I was initially worried about scratching my paint, but this towel glided smoothly and left a streak-free finish. Even after several washes, it remains soft and gentle. Streak-Free Drying: This is perhaps the most important factor, and the Blackline towel delivers. I've been very impressed with how effectively it dries my car without leaving streaks or water spots. Quality: The construction feels solid and durable. The stitching seems well done, and the towel has held up well through multiple washes. It feels like a premium product. Cons: Price: Compared to some other microfiber towels, the Blackline is a bit more expensive. However, in my opinion, the performance and quality justify the cost. You get what you pay for. Care: Like most high-quality microfiber towels, it requires specific care. You need to avoid fabric softeners and wash it separately to maintain its absorbency and softness. This isn't a huge drawback, but something to be aware of. Read more Read less
items.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2025
